zh.lifeSummary: 當Margarete von Berg於大約1364年出生在海因里希_海普洛文次爾利時，她的父親Wilhelm II. von Berg18歲，母親Anna Von Bayern19歲。她於1379年與Otto I. von Braunschweig-Göttingen在海因里希_海普洛文次爾利結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1442年7月18日在德國尼德薩辛藍德可萊斯 諾特海姆哈德森享年79歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margarete von Berg于大约1364年出生在海因里希_海普洛文次爾利时，她的父亲Wilhelm II. von Berg18岁，母亲Anna Von Bayern19岁。她于1379年与Otto I. von Braunschweig-Göttingen在海因里希_海普洛文次爾利结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1442年7月18日在德国尼德薩辛藍德可萊斯 諾特海姆哈德森享年79岁。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Berg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lennart, Bjorn, Lars, Nils, Alf, Nels, Thor, Tor, Knute, Selmer, Anders.

German and Dutch: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill or mountain, from Middle High German berc, Middle Dutch berghe. This surname is also found in some other parts of Europe, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Poland. Compare Dutch Vandenberg .
Norwegian and Danish: habitational name from a farmstead named with Old Norse bjarg ‘mountain, hill’.

variants.name: Berger
meanings: German, Dutch, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic name for someone who lived in the mountains or hills (see   Berg  ). The surname of German origin is also found in many other European countries, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Russia, Poland, Czechia, Hungary, and Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of corresponding Slavic topographic names or surnames. As a Jewish name it is mainly artificial. Compare   Bargar  ,   Barger  ,   Barker  ,   Barrier  , and   Barriger  .
French: occupational name from Old French bergier ‘shepherd’ (from Late Latin berbicarius, from berbex ‘ram’). It is also found in England, as a surname of Huguenot origin. Compare Shepard .
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farms so named with the plural of   Berg   ‘mountain’.

variants.name: Bergh
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Johan, Anders, Arndt, Owe, Per, Sigvard, Sven, Syvert.
Scandinavian: variant of   Berg  .
Dutch and English: topographic name denoting someone living on or by a hill, Middle Dutch berghe, Middle English bergh ‘hill’ (Old English beorg). Compare Dutch Berg and Vandenbergh .

variants.name: Vandenberg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Dirk, Marinus, Gerrit, Gerritt, Hendrik, Jacobus, Maarten, Pieter, Wim, Albertus, Durk, Hendrick.
Dutch (Van den Berg) and Flemish (also Van den Berg): topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill, Middle Dutch berghe. Compare Berg .

variants.name: Berglin
meanings: Swedish: variant of Berg + the suffix -lin, derived from Latin -(l)inius ‘relating to’.

variants.name: Berget
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Knut.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farm names especially in eastern Norway named with the definite singular form of berg ‘mountain, hill’ (see Berg ).
French: topographic name, a derivative of   Berge   ‘river bank’.

variants.name: Baerg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Kurt.
German (Bärg): variant of Berg .

variants.name: Bergen
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from any of various places called Bergen.
Dutch and Flemish: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill (see   Berg  ), or a habitational name from any of various places so named (see   Van Bergen  ).
Swedish (mainly Bergén): ornamental or habitational name formed with the (placename) element berg ‘mountain, hill’ + the adjectival suffix -en/-én, a derivative of Latin -enius ‘relating to’.

variants.name: Berghorst
meanings: Dutch (Gelderland): topographic name for someone living on or by a hill with shrubberies (see   Berg   and   Horst  ).

variants.name: Barg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Bernhard, Erwin, Hans, Mathias, Wilfried.
North German:
topographic name for someone who lived on a hill, Low German barg. Compare Berg .

variants.name: Berge
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Maren, Johan, Karsten, Alf, Bent, Erik, Gudmund, Knud, Peer, Per, Sten, Thor.
Norwegian and Swedish: variant of   Berg  , from the dative form. As a Norwegian surname, this is a habitational name from any of numerous farms so named all over the country.
German: topographic name for a person by or on a hill, from an extended form of Berg (formerly with a preposition with dative case, e.g. am Berge).

nameMeanings.name: Margarete
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from Greek. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaret
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretha
meanings: from Greek. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margarethe
meanings: from Greek. See Margaret .

en.lifeSummary: When Margarete von Berg was born about 1364, in Jülich, Rhineland, Prussia, her father, Wilhelm II. von Berg, was 18 and her mother, Anna Von Bayern, was 19. She married Otto I. von Braunschweig-Göttingen in 1379, in Jülich, Rhineland, Prussia. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 18 July 1442, in Hardegsen, Northeim, Lower Saxony, Germany, at the age of 79.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margarete von Berg est née environ 1364, à Jülich, Rhénanie, Prusse, son père, Wilhelm II. von Berg, avait 18 ans et sa mère, Anna Von Bayern, avait 19 ans. Elle a épousé Otto I. von Braunschweig-Göttingen en 1379, à Jülich, Rhénanie, Prusse. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 18 juillet 1442, à Hardegsen, Landkreis Northeim, Niedersachsen, Allemagne, à l’âge de 79 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margarete von Berg um 1364 in Jülich, Rheinprovinz, Preußen geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Wilhelm II. von Berg 18 und ihre Mutter Anna Von Bayern 19. Sie hat 1379 in Jülich, Rheinprovinz, Preußen Otto I. von Braunschweig-Göttingen geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 18. Juli 1442 im Alter von 79 Jahren in Hardegsen, Landkreis Northeim, Niedersachsen, Deutschland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margarete von Berg è nata il circa 1364 nel Jülich, Rheinprovinz, Preußen, sua padre, Wilhelm II. von Berg, aveva 18 anni e sua madre, Anna Von Bayern, aveva 19 anni. Lei sposato Otto I. von Braunschweig-Göttingen nel 1379, nel Jülich, Rheinprovinz, Preußen. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 18 luglio 1442, nel Hardegsen, Landkreis Northeim, Niedersachsen, Germania, all'età di 79 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margarete von Bergが約1364年にプレウssンルヘインプロヴィンズジuリチで生まれた時，父Wilhelm II. von Bergは18歳，母Anna Von Bayernは19歳であった。1379年にプレウssンルヘインプロヴィンズジuリチでOtto I. von Braunschweig-Göttingenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1442年7月18日にドイツニーダーサチセンランドクレイス ノースエイムハーデグセンで79歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margarete von Berg(이)가 대략 1364년 프레우ss 리인프로빈츠 ju리치에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Wilhelm II. von Berg(은)는 18세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anna Von Bayern(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 1379년 프레우ss 리인프로빈츠 ju리치에서 Otto I. von Braunschweig-Göttingen(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1442년 7월 18일 독일 니더작센 랜드크레이스 노르테임 하르데그센에서 79세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margarete von Berg nasceu aproximadamente 1364, em Jülich, Rheinprovinz, Preußen, seu pai, Wilhelm II. von Berg, tinha 18 anos e sua mãe, Anna Von Bayern, tinha 19 anos. Ela casou-se com Otto I. von Braunschweig-Göttingen em 1379, em Jülich, Rheinprovinz, Preußen. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 18 de julho de 1442, em Hardegsen, Landkreis Northeim, Niedersachsen, Alemanha, com 79 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margarete von Berg родилась около 1364 г. в местности джuлич, раинпровинз, Пруссия, её отцу по имени Wilhelm II. von Berg было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Anna Von Bayern, 19. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Otto I. von Braunschweig-Göttingen в 1379 г. в местности джuлич, раинпровинз, Пруссия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 18 июля 1442 г. в местности хардегсен, ландкрис нортим, ниедирсачсен, Германия в возрасте 79 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margarete von Berg nació aproximadamente 1364, en Jülich, Rheinprovinz, Preußen, su papá, Wilhelm II. von Berg, tenía 18 años y su mamá, Anna Von Bayern, tenía 19 años. Se casó con Otto I. von Braunschweig-Göttingen en 1379, en Jülich, Rheinprovinz, Preußen. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 18 de julio de 1442, en Hardegsen, Landkreis Northeim, Niedersachsen, Alemania, a la edad de 79 años.
